channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Defense Argues No Evidence Links Sara Duterte to Assassin Hiring in Marcos Case
item.description: The defense team of Vice President Sara Duterte asserted during the impeachment trial that the House prosecution lacks sufficient evidence to prove she hired an assassin to kill President Ferdinand 'B...

item.title: Switzerland's Historic World Cup Run Enters Quarter-Finals
item.description: Switzerland made history by advancing to the World Cup quarter-finals for the first time since 1954, defeating Colombia 4-3 on penalties after a goalless draw in Vancouver. The victory sparked nationw...

item.title: Tanzania experiences calm day as July 7 passes without protests
item.description: Calm prevailed across Tanzania on July 7, 2026, as citizens continued daily activities without major incidents. Despite social media rumors about potential protests, police maintained visible security...

item.title: Trump Mistakenly References 'Islamic Republic of Japan' During NATO Summit
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump made a notable error during a NATO summit meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy in Ankara, Turkey, on July 8, 2026. While discussing recent security threats,...

item.title: FilOil Cargo's Leadership Secures Letran's Eighth Win; San Beda Claims Second Seed
item.description: The article highlights a significant milestone in the UAAP basketball season as FilOil Cargo led Letran to their eighth consecutive victory, solidifying their dominance in the league. San Beda Univers...

item.title: Tanzania's Colonial Heritage in European Museums and the Fight for Cultural Ownership
item.description: The article discusses how Tanzania's colonial heritage, including artifacts and historical records, remains scattered across European museums and private collections. A Slovenian exhibition, 'Tanzania...

item.title: Access Blocked: Instructions for Resolving Bot Detection Issue
item.description: News Corp Australia has implemented software to manage crawler bot traffic on their websites. Users who are not bots but encounter access issues should follow specific steps to resolve the problem. Th...

item.title: Trump Legal Team Refuses to Pay E. Jean Carroll $5 Million in Damages
item.description: President Donald Trump's legal team continues to refuse paying E. Jean Carroll the $5 million damages verdict awarded after a 2023 jury found him liable for sexual abuse and defamation. Despite the Su...

item.title: Deputy Governors Lead Northern Forum Amid Security and Economic Pressures
item.description: The Northern States Governors’ Forum (NSGF) held its meeting in Kaduna, with a significant number of deputy governors attending instead of the state governors. Out of the 19 northern states expected, ...

item.title: PM Modi Visits 9th-Century Hindu Temple in Indonesia
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi recently visited the ancient Prambanan temple in Indonesia, a 9th-century Hindu architectural marvel. The temple, dedicated to the Hindu trinity of Brahma, Vishnu, and Shi...

item.title: Cybersecurity Salaries in Kenya 2026: Ranges and Career Opportunities
item.description: The article details cybersecurity salary trends in Kenya for 2026, highlighting varying pay scales based on roles, experience, and location. Entry-level professionals earn between KES 80,000 to 150,00...

item.title: South African Monarchs to Address Regional Migration Tensions with Zimbabwe
item.description: The article details how King Ndamase of AmaMpondo is leading a regional diplomatic initiative to address growing migration tensions between South Africa and Zimbabwe. Acting as chair of the Kings Foru...

item.title: Trump Ends Ceasefire with Iran, Cites Failed Negotiations and Escalating Tensions
item.description: President Donald Trump has officially ended the US-Iran ceasefire, calling further talks with Tehran a waste of time. The decision comes after the US launched strikes on over 80 Iranian targets in res...

item.title: Rhodes Scholarship Opportunities and Application Tips for Southern African Students
item.description: The Rhodes Trust has issued practical guidance for prospective applicants seeking the prestigious scholarship to study at the University of Oxford. Ten fully funded scholarships are available for exce...

item.title: Study Reveals Men Twice as Likely to Hit Wall in Marathons Compared to Women
item.description: A recent study analyzing 870,000 marathon times has uncovered a significant gender disparity in marathon performance. Men are nearly twice as likely as women to experience a 'catastrophic deceleration...

item.title: Residents Of Enugu Deputy Governor's Community Struggle With Contaminated Water Source
item.description: Residents of Urukpa Ezimo Community in Udenu Local Government Area, Enugu State — the hometown of Deputy Governor Ifeanyi Ossai — are facing a severe public health crisis due to lack of clean water. D...

item.title: Senate impeachment court schedules oral arguments on VP Sara Duterte's tax records subpoena
item.description: The Senate impeachment court has scheduled oral arguments for July 15 to address the House prosecution's request to subpoena the tax records of Vice President Sara Duterte, her husband Manases Carpio,...

item.title: Government and Private Sector Announce New Oil Pipeline Projects and Production Growth in Alberta
item.description: The article discusses recent government and private sector announcements signaling potential growth in Alberta's oil industry. Federal and Alberta governments have committed to a new West Coast oil pi...

item.title: Tragic Bus Accident in Tabora Claims Six Lives, 11 Injured
item.description: A severe bus accident occurred in the Chemchem area of Tabora region on Tuesday, July 7, 2026, resulting in six fatalities, including a young child, and 11 injuries. The incident involved a Last Bulle...

item.title: NCERT revises Class 8 textbook by removing judiciary corruption chapter
item.description: The National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT) has revised its Class 8 textbook, removing sections that discussed 'corruption in the judiciary' following Supreme Court directives. T...
